title: [Sentry ERROR] DriveError: Network error: Load failed (www.googleapis.com)
body:
**Sentry Error Report** (auto-created by pipeline)

**Error:** DriveError: Network error: Load failed (www.googleapis.com)
**Location:** Mo(babybloom/assets/index-CWxGLerX)
**Level:** error
**Events:** 1 | **Users affected:** 0
**First seen:** 2026-04-01T08:46:41.350000Z
**Last seen:** 2026-04-01T08:46:41.350000Z
**Sentry link:** https://self-6iw.sentry.io/issues/7379432376/
**Sentry ID:** 7379432376

---
_This issue was auto-created from a Sentry error report. Fix the root cause and the Sentry issue will be auto-resolved on deploy._
enhanced_description:
What the user reported: A Sentry error captures a DriveError with message 'Network error: Load failed (www.googleapis.com)'. This means a fetch/XHR request to the Google Drive API failed at the network level — not an auth or API error, but the TCP/HTTP connection itself failed. On iOS/Safari, 'Load failed' is the standard error thrown when a network request is interrupted, the device is offline, or a request times out.

Component affected: Google Drive sync module (src/ — likely the drive utility/service file that makes API calls to googleapis.com).

Expected behaviour: When a network request to Google Drive fails due to connectivity issues, the app should catch the error gracefully, show a user-friendly offline/retry message, and NOT surface an unhandled DriveError to Sentry.

Current behaviour: The network failure propagates as an unhandled DriveError, which gets captured by Sentry as an error-level event.

Edge cases to handle:
- Device is fully offline
- Device has intermittent / poor connectivity
- Request times out mid-flight
- Privacy settings or firewall blocks googleapis.com
- Safari-specific 'Load failed' on backgrounded tabs

Acceptance criteria:
1. Network failures during Drive API calls are caught and classified as transient (not bugs)
2. User sees a friendly message (e.g. 'Sync unavailable — check your connection') rather than a crash
3. The error is NOT reported to Sentry (or reported at 'warning' level at most)
4. Sync retries or degrades gracefully to local-only mode

Testing: Add unit test that mocks a network failure (fetch rejection with a TypeError 'Load failed') in the Drive sync code and verifies the error is caught gracefully. Add a regression test ensuring no unhandled promise rejection occurs when the Drive API is unreachable.
implementation_notes:
WHAT YOU ACTUALLY DID:
- Files changed:
  * src/lib/sync/syncEngine.ts: Added 'network' to skipCodes set so network-level DriveErrors are not reported to Sentry (they are transient connectivity issues, not code bugs)
  * src/lib/sync/googleDrive.ts: (1) Replaced bare fetch() in refreshAccessToken with fetchWithTimeout() so network failures during token refresh are caught and converted to DriveError(network,...) rather than propagating as raw TypeErrors; (2) Added case 'network' to DriveError.userMessage with user-friendly text
- Tests added:
  * tests/unit/driveNetworkError.test.ts: Covers 'network' in skipCodes, fetchWithTimeout converts network errors correctly, refreshAccessToken uses fetchWithTimeout (not bare fetch), DriveError.userMessage for 'network'
- Approach taken:
  Two root causes were identified and fixed:
  (1) 'network' DriveErrorCode was missing from skipCodes in triggerSync() - fixed by adding it
  (2) refreshAccessToken() used bare fetch() without network-error handling, so iOS 'Load failed' TypeErrors would bypass DriveError classification and go to Sentry - fixed by switching to fetchWithTimeout()
  The fetchWithTimeout function already correctly converts network errors to DriveError('network',...) for all other call sites.
- Trade-offs: None significant - network errors are definitively transient and user-facing message is already shown via syncStatus.errorMessage in the UI
